_note: Dual-position hybrids per 2026 analyst scouting. primary = draft-listed position (predictions_2026.csv consensus); flex = realistic alternate role analysts explicitly project. Evidence cites analyst or aggregated internal reasoning (big_board_reasoning_2026 = internal aggregation of PFF/Brugler/Jeremiah/Zierlein/ESPN). Only includes prospects with explicit dual-role scouting language; pure single-position players excluded.

Arvell Reese.evidence: Brugler/Jeremiah: chess-piece defender deployable as LB, EDGE, and QB spy; 22 pressures on 71 pass-rush snaps (PFF); scheme fits listed as '3-4 OLB / 4-3 SAM / hybrid multiple' (big_board_reasoning)

Sonny Styles.evidence: Brugler: 'tall rangy LB transitioned from safety'; Jeremiah comp Isaiah Simmons; mirrors TEs and carries receivers downfield; Ohio State box/nickel usage confirmed

Caleb Downs.evidence: NFL.com (Zierlein) and Jeremiah: 'positionless' — single-high FS, big-nickel, quarters, over-slot; comp Kyle Hamilton; listed scheme fits include 'positionless' (big_board_reasoning)

Avieon Terrell.evidence: PFF / Brugler: undersized outside (180 lbs), matches in slot, stellar blitzer, big-nickel fit; 'nickel corner / blitz-capable slot' per big_board_reasoning

Eli Stowers.evidence: Zierlein / Brugler: former QB-turned-WR-turned-TE; 386 YAC 2nd among FBS TEs; 'move TE / flex' scheme fit; lines up in slot heavily

Kenyon Sadiq.evidence: Brugler: primarily attached or slot; Jeremiah comp George Kittle/Evan Engram; 'move TE, Y in 12 personnel' — genuine flex TE

Max Klare.evidence: PFF: best route-running in class; 'slot TE' archetype; 'flex TE, pass-heavy offense' scheme fit — projects to align like a big slot

Michael Trigg.evidence: PFF/Brugler: basketball frame / 7-foot wingspan, 'move TE, red-zone target' scheme fit; heavy slot usage at Baylor

Justin Joly.evidence: Brugler: move-TE projection; physical catch-point; back-to-back 43+ reception seasons; lines up flex/slot

Anthony Hill Jr..evidence: Brugler: 17 sacks + 8 FFs over three years; 'superb blitzer'; Devin White comp; pattern-match coverage LB with edge-rush sub-package value

Jacob Rodriguez.evidence: PFF: former safety converted to LB; 'outstanding lateral range', 'zone-drop awareness'; punch-away tackling — hybrid LB/S deployment

Jaishawn Barham.evidence: Brugler: 'four sacks pass-rush glimpses', scheme fit 'SAM/hybrid'; Michigan used him as off-ball + stand-up rusher

Kyle Louis.evidence: Brugler/PFF: 'ultra-versatile (box, LB, slot, deep)'; comp Kyle Hamilton (position-flex); scheme fits 'hybrid S/LB, big-nickel'; positionless chess piece

Jake Golday.evidence: PFF: 'edge-rusher size with safety athleticism'; archetype tag 'hybrid LB/edge'; WILL/SAM/coverage LB scheme range

Harold Perkins.evidence: Jeremiah/PFF: originally an edge-rusher at LSU; classic Jalon Walker-style sub-package pass rusher who drops into coverage; usage spans SAM/nickel-edge

Bryce Boettcher.evidence: Zierlein/PFN: Oregon safety-to-LB convert, 2-sport (baseball) athlete; sub-package big-nickel role projected

Dillon Thieneman.evidence: Brugler: 'versatile chess piece (box, deep half, over slot)'; scheme fits 'quarters, big-nickel, FS in two-high'; Hufanga comp

Emmanuel McNeil-Warren.evidence: PFF/Brugler: 'best box safety in class', 11 career FFs; scheme fit 'strong safety, box safety, big-nickel'; Jamal Adams enforcer comp — regular LB-depth alignment

Kamari Ramsey.evidence: Zierlein/PFF: ex-UCLA nickel who moved to safety at USC; projects as big-nickel / slot-safety hybrid

Jalon Kilgore.evidence: Brugler: South Carolina 'Star' position — slot agility + run-defense physicality; scheme fits 'big-nickel, slot-safety hybrid'

Bud Clark.evidence: Brugler: 'slot-receiver and deep coverage capability'; scheme fit 'FS, big-nickel, deep-half' — six-season veteran with slot snaps

Genesis Smith.evidence: PFF: Arizona enforcer-frame safety, heavy box usage; projects as big-nickel dime LB per internal archetype 'split_safety' + Jeremiah blurb

Robert Spears-Jennings.evidence: Brugler/Zierlein: Oklahoma strong safety regularly aligned as dime LB; 220+ lb frame; box/nickel hybrid projection

VJ Payne.evidence: PFF: Kansas State versatile DB; played FS + nickel CB snaps; split-safety archetype with slot flex

Zakee Wheatley.evidence: Brugler: Penn State split-safety who plays big-nickel snaps; sub-package linebacker overlay

Michael Taaffe.evidence: Brugler/Jeremiah: Texas STAR position — slot-nickel coverage + deep-half; classic Iowa/Jim Thorpe-finalist slot-safety archetype

A.J. Haulcy.evidence: Brugler: 'in-the-box SS', four 10-tackle games; physical run-supporting safety with dime-LB projection

Keionte Scott.evidence: Brugler: 'excellent force player and blitzer'; scheme fits 'nickel CB, big-nickel safety, blitz package'; Desmond King comp — true CB/safety hybrid

D'angelo Ponds.evidence: PFF: 5'9 slot-only profile; 'outside-slot versatility'; Kenny Moore II comp — nickel/slot hybrid that can rotate to sub-package safety

Keith Abney II.evidence: Brugler: 'outside-slot versatility'; scheme fits 'nickel, outside-slot hybrid' — projected nickel/sub-safety role

Lorenzo Styles Jr..evidence: Zierlein/Jeremiah: former Notre Dame/Ohio State WR who converted to CB; rare two-way flex; still lines up occasionally in slot on offense in goal-line packages

Chandler Rivers.evidence: Brugler: 5'10 185 plant-and-drive; size limits outside matchups; slot-nickel kick projection consistent across PFF/NFL.com boards

Keldric Faulk.evidence: Brugler: 'versatile DL with ideal frame'; scheme fits 'multi-front, 5-tech DE, 3-4 DE, interior pass rusher in nickel'; kick-inside candidate

T.J. Parker.evidence: Brugler: 'versatile (edge and interior)'; scheme fits '4-3 base end, 3-4 DE, inside-outside hybrid'; Larry Ogunjobi comp

Zion Young.evidence: Brugler: 'can slide inside in sub-packages'; scheme fit '4-3 base end, inside-outside in sub' — true base-end / sub-3T hybrid

Rueben Bain Jr..evidence: Brugler: 'DT-ish build for edge', 'can kick inside on obvious pass'; scheme fit includes 'can kick inside on obvious pass' — explicit sub-package interior flex

Gabe Jacas.evidence: Brugler: 'seals edge and drops into coverage'; archetype 'coverage-capable LB/edge hybrid'; scheme fits 3-4 OLB / 4-3 SAM

R Mason Thomas.evidence: Brugler: 'lacks ideal height/length/bulk'; scheme fits '3-4 OLB, stand-up edge, sub-package rusher' — stand-up tweener

Romello Height.evidence: Brugler: 'sub-240 frame'; scheme fit '3-4 OLB, sub-package rusher' — off-ball SAM fallback

Patrick Payton.evidence: PFF/Zierlein: long-framed LSU stand-up rusher; 3-4 OLB with drop-coverage snaps, SAM flex

Joshua Josephs.evidence: Brugler: 83⅞" wingspan at lighter weight; scheme fits '3-4 OLB / 4-3 DE' — length-based edge with kick-inside utility in sub

Peter Woods.evidence: Brugler: 'lateral range outside tackle box'; scheme fits '3-tech, 1-tech in base, multiple-front'; Christian Barmore comp — interior who slides to 5T

Caleb Banks.evidence: Brugler: tall long 85.75" wingspan DT; scheme fits '3-tech, multi-front' — length-based interior with occasional 4i-5T alignment

Gracen Halton.evidence: Brugler: 'gap-attack specialty'; scheme fit '3-tech, sub-package penetrator' — pure sub-package pass rusher

Chris McClellan.evidence: Brugler: 'versatile front player'; scheme fit '3-tech, multi-front' — 3T/5T flex interior

Spencer Fano.evidence: Brugler/Jeremiah: 'five-position versatility'; took center snaps at Combine; Zack Martin guard projection comp; scheme fit explicitly 'interior OL'

Kadyn Proctor.evidence: Brugler: 'pass-protection limits likely kick inside'; archetype 'kick-inside candidate'; 360+ lb frame screams guard at next level

Caleb Tiernan.evidence: Brugler: 'potential guard projection if taller-rusher management doesn't improve'; archetype 'OT/OG swing'

Gennings Dunker.evidence: Brugler: 37 of 38 starts at RT but 'guard transition potential'; archetype 'RT-to-G swing'

Chase Bisontis.evidence: Brugler: multi-position starter (LG, RG, RT); archetype 'versatile IOL' with swing-tackle experience

Logan Jones.evidence: Brugler: DL-to-OL conversion at Iowa; unique two-way background informs stunt/blitz recognition; primarily center now

Parker Brailsford.evidence: Zierlein: Alabama C/G flex; swing-OL experience across interior spots (center primary, guard fallback)

Connor Lew.evidence: Brugler: best-center prospect but college snaps at guard/tackle fill-in; archetype tags 'interior_OL' + versatility (prospect_archetypes)

KC Concepcion.evidence: Brugler: 'punt-return contributor'; comp Tank Dell / Rondale Moore; used on jet-sweep / RB touches at A&M — gadget slot-RB flex

Zachariah Branch.evidence: Brugler: 'return-game versatility', 'jet motion, RPO' scheme fit; Zay Flowers / Jaylen Waddle comp — regularly lined up as RB/slot gadget at USC/Georgia

Makai Lemon.evidence: Brugler: 'RB body + LB temperament'; comp Cooper Kupp / Amon-Ra St. Brown; jet-sweep / backfield gadget usage at USC

Antonio Williams.evidence: Brugler: 'feels soft zones', 'quick-game / RPO'; slot WR with gadget-RB usage at Clemson — Tyler Lockett / Rashee Rice comp

Jeremiyah Love.evidence: Jeremiah/Brugler: Notre Dame RB with receiver-caliber route running; 3-down pass-catching flex, motion WR usage in 11 personnel

Jonah Coleman.evidence: Brugler: 'power_back + pass_catching_back' archetype; Washington slot/motion WR packages — legitimate third-down flex

Emmett Johnson.evidence: Brugler: 'elite receiving production (85 catches over two seasons)'; Chase Brown comp; 'pass-game RB' scheme fit — true pass-catching flex
